name: Pasta Pizza
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon dried hot red pepper flakes
1/2 small green bell pepper, sliced into thin rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the sauce: In a saucepan, cook the garlic in the oil over moderately low heat, stirring until it is just golden.
2. Add the tomatoes (chopped), onion, basil, red pepper flakes, oregano, sugar, and salt to taste. Bring the mixture to a boil and simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and almost all liquid has evaporated.
3. While the sauce cooks, in boiling salted water, cook the capellini for 6 to 8 minutes or until tender, then drain well.
4. In a non-stick skillet measuring 10 inches across, heat oil over moderate heat until hot but not smoking.
5. Cook the bell pepper rings for 30 seconds on each side, until crisp-tender, and transfer with tongs to a plate.
6. Add the capellini to the skillet, distributing evenly and pressing lightly. Cook covered for 25 minutes or until golden.
7. Invert an oiled baking sheet over the skillet; invert the capellini crust onto the sheet. Slide back into the skillet.
8. Cover and cook the crust for 10 more minutes, spreading sauce over it (leaving a 1/2-inch border) and arranging mozzarella on top.
9. Arrange bell pepper rings and pepperoni on the mozzarella; cover and cook until melted, about 3 minutes.
10. Slide the pizza onto a pizza pan or flat platter. Garnish with basil. Cut into wedges.
Yield: 2 servings
